summary: Psychic Wayne is a Southern California astrology and tarot reader offering phone and online readings in 10-to-60-minute sessions, plus digital reports (natal chart, numerology, compatibility, transit) and tarot products via the website and Etsy. He serves spiritually curious people — primarily women 25–55 — seeking personalized clarity on love, relationships, career, and major life decisions. Bookings are one-off and self-serve through Acuity Scheduling or a direct phone line, with repeat visits driven by new life questions rather than any subscription.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Turn a brand-new email or VIP-SMS subscriber into their first booked reading by getting them to claim the free 20-minute session — the fastest path to the 'aha' moment.
campaigns.trigger: New subscriber joins the email list, opts in for astro updates, or texts FUTURE to the VIP SMS line. Send within seconds.
campaigns.segment: All new contacts who have not yet booked a session; lead with the free 20-minute reading regardless of source.
campaigns.rationale: Wayne's whole business turns on the first live reading where he names something accurate the client never shared — so the single highest-leverage job is getting a curious new subscriber to actually book that first session while their interest is hot.

emails.purpose: Warm, mystical welcome that sets expectations and delivers one quick win — claiming the free 20-minute reading — before skepticism or distraction sets in.
emails.subject: Your free 20-minute reading is waiting, [First Name]
emails.preview: The cards already have something to tell you. Let's not keep them waiting.
keyPoints: Open with a warm, personal welcome in Wayne's voice and name the outcome they came for: clarity on love, career, or a decision they can't make alone
Single primary action — book the free 20-minute tarot reading via Acuity (psychicwayne1.as.me); one button, no competing links
Establish credibility lightly: third-generation reader, 20+ years, trusted by A-list clients — enough to ease the 'is this real?' doubt without bragging
Set expectations for the next few emails (short, useful, no spam) and invite a real reply; close with the entertainment-purposes disclaimer

campaigns.name: Free Reading → Paid Session Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert someone who just had the free 20-minute reading into a paid 30- or 60-minute session while the resonance of the 'how did he know that?' moment is still fresh.
campaigns.trigger: Free 20-minute reading is completed; send same day, within a few hours of the session.
campaigns.segment: Contacts who completed a free reading and have not yet booked a paid session; branch by the theme they came in with (love vs. career vs. life direction).
campaigns.rationale: The free reading is the trial, and this brand's emotionally-driven, same-day cycle means the window to upsell is hours, not weeks — a single well-timed 'we only scratched the surface' email is the core revenue play.

emails.purpose: Strike while the connection is hot — reinforce the accuracy they just experienced and offer the deeper paid session as the natural next step to a full answer.
emails.subject: We only scratched the surface today
emails.preview: Twenty minutes opened the door. A full reading walks you through it.
keyPoints: Acknowledge what surfaced in their free reading and validate the resonance — they felt it was real, so name that the cards had more to say than 20 minutes allowed
Frame the upgrade by outcome, not minutes: a 30- or 60-minute session to fully work through the love, career, or decision they came in for
Reduce friction — direct Acuity booking link plus the phone line (1-888-929-6307) for those who'd rather talk to a person
Gently address price sensitivity by tying cost to the weight of the decision; include the entertainment-purposes disclaimer

campaigns.name: Booking, Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended sessions and recover the bookings that slip, protecting revenue that's already on the calendar.
campaigns.trigger: A session is booked via Acuity; reminder cadence fires before the appointment, with a separate no-show recovery branch.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled phone or online reading; no-show branch targets those who miss their slot.
campaigns.rationale: Readings are time-blocked appointments Wayne delivers live, so every no-show is lost revenue he can't resell — reminders cut no-shows by roughly 35%, making this the fastest way to protect bookings already won.

emails.purpose: The 24-hour reminder — the highest-impact send for reducing no-shows; confirm the time, prep them to get the most from the session, and make rescheduling effortless.
emails.subject: Your reading with Wayne is tomorrow at [time]
emails.preview: Come with the one question that's been weighing on you.
keyPoints: Confirm date, time, session length, and how the reading happens (phone or online) so there's zero ambiguity about showing up
Help them prepare — encourage them to come with their key question about love, career, or a decision; a focused question makes the reading land harder
One-tap reschedule link to recover the slot rather than lose it to a silent no-show
Warm, reassuring close in Wayne's voice plus the entertainment-purposes disclaimer

campaigns.name: Free Reading Page — Lead Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move free-20-minute-reading-page leads who signed up but never booked toward their first session by dismantling the core objection: skepticism about whether readings are real.
campaigns.trigger: Visitor opts in on the free reading landing page but does not book a session within a few days; gentle every-few-days cadence.
campaigns.segment: Leads who claimed or expressed interest in the free reading but have not booked; branch by stated topic of interest if known.
campaigns.rationale: The #1 thing stopping these warm-but-hesitant leads is 'is this actually real or will it help me?', so the nurture's hero send must answer that with real client experiences rather than more promises.

emails.purpose: Handle the skepticism objection head-on with proof — real client stories of accurate, specific guidance — so a curious-but-doubtful lead feels safe enough to book.
emails.subject: "How did he know that?" — what first-timers always say
emails.preview: You don't have to believe yet. Just bring one question.
emails.cta: See what a reading can tell you
keyPoints: Lead with the doubt out loud — it's okay to be skeptical; most clients were too before their first reading
Share two or three short, specific client moments where Wayne named something accurate the person never shared — concrete beats generic
Lower the stakes by pointing back to the free 20-minute reading as a no-risk way to judge for themselves
Soft, low-pressure CTA to book the free reading; close with the entertainment-purposes disclaimer

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate past clients and cold subscribers who haven't booked in 60–90+ days, using a timely celestial event as the reason to return.
campaigns.trigger: 60–90 days of no booking or no email engagement; ideally timed to a New Moon, eclipse, Mercury retrograde, or the contact's birthday.
campaigns.segment: Dormant past clients and cold VIP/email subscribers; reference what they came in for last time where known.
campaigns.rationale: This brand's repeat revenue is purely client-driven and clusters around life transitions and astrological moments, so a win-back anchored to a real celestial event gives a lapsed client a natural, non-salesy reason to rebook.

emails.purpose: Reopen the relationship by tying a current astrological event to a fresh reason to check in — re-establish the value and invite an easy return.
emails.subject: Mercury retrograde is back — and so are your questions
emails.preview: It's been a while. The stars have shifted. Maybe you have too.
keyPoints: Warm 'it's been a while' open that references the current celestial event (retrograde / eclipse / new moon) as a meaningful moment to take stock
Remind them of the core value — clarity and direction when life feels uncertain again — and, if known, nod to what they last came in for
Make returning effortless with a direct Acuity booking link and the phone option
Keep it low-pressure and caring rather than salesy; include the entertainment-purposes disclaimer
